questions.question: Solve for x: 3x + 2 < 11
questions.explanation: Subtract 2 from both sides: 3x < 9. Divide by 3: x < 3. The inequality sign stays the same because we divided by a positive number.

questions.question: Which of the following values of y satisfies y + 5 ≥ 12? List all that apply: 6, 7, 8, 9
questions.explanation: Subtract 5 from both sides: y ≥ 7. Check each: 6 < 7 (no), 7 = 7 (yes, but not listed separately), 8 > 7 (yes), 9 > 7 (yes). Values 8 and 9 work.

questions.question: Solve the inequality: 2x - 4 > 6
questions.explanation: Add 4 to both sides: 2x > 10. Divide both sides by 2: x > 5.

questions.question: On a number line, graph the solution to x ≤ 2. Should the circle at 2 be filled or unfilled?
questions.explanation: The inequality x ≤ 2 includes the value 2 (because of the equal sign), so use a filled circle at 2 and shade left.

questions.question: A video store charges $3 per rental. If you have $20 to spend, write an inequality for the maximum number of videos n you can rent.
questions.explanation: Each video costs $3, so n videos cost 3n dollars. You can spend at most $20, so 3n ≤ 20.

questions.question: Solve: -2x > 8
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by -2. When dividing by a negative number, flip the inequality sign: x < -4.
